rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 6, RESPONDENT T. YUEN, CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Defendants' demurrer is OFF CALENDAR as moot as to the claim for petition for writ of mandate. The court denied the writ on July 20, 2018. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the declaratory relief claim. No opposition filed and the cause of action is unnecessary. (See CCP 1061.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Depositions Of Retained Experts Designated By Defendants And Cross Complainants Yunji Willa Qian And Qian And Company Meet And Confer Declaration Of John T Bachmayer And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT YUNJI QIAN, QIAN & COMPANY, INC. Motion For Protective Order Re Depositions Of Retained Experts Designated By Defendants And Cross Complainants Yunji Willa Qian And Qian And Company. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Local Rules state: "Failure to lodge courtesy copies of opposition Papers as required by statute, rule or court order may, in the discretion of the judicial officer presiding over the hearing, result in the granting of the motion or continuance of the hearing." S.F. Civ. L.R. 2.7(B)(4). Further "Any counsel, party represented by counsel, or self represented litigant, who fails to comply with any of the requirements set forth in the rules will, upon motion of a party or the Court, be subject to the sanctions set forth in CCP § 575.2." S.F. Civ. L.R. 1.1. Here, the Court infers from the filing of Defendant Yunji Willa Qian's and Qian & Company, Inc.'s (collectively "Qian's") Reply paper that Plaintiff Qian & Nemecek LLP ("Qian & Nemecek") served but did not courtesy copy (and perhaps did not file) opposition papers. Although under Local Rules the Court is authorized to grant the motion without further briefing, it declines to do so at this time. Instead Quian & Nemeck must deliver courtesy copies of its opposition papers to Judge Pro Tem B. Douglas Robbins at 1 Post St. Suite 800, San Francisco, CA 94104, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 2, 2018, at 5:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per California Rules of Court 3.1110(f). At the hearing, the court will hear substantive arguments as well as arguments, if any, as to why the motion should be continued. Parties are ordered to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NASER ODEH VS. ISMAEL KARAJAH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NASER ODEH's Motion To Enforce Settlement. Plaintiff Odeh's "motion for entry of judgment pursuant to terms of stipulated settlement" is DENIED. Plaintiff demands that defendant be given only "seven days" to "carry out all the terms of the settlement." However, one of the settlement's key terms is that defendant pay "all outstanding governmental tax liens." At least one tax lien cannot be paid at present because state tax authorities have yet to provide the required clearance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN JOSE SANCHEZ VS. DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Doyle's Work Company Inc.'s ("DWC") motion for summary judgment is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether DWC was performing excavation work in the accident area the day before the accident occurred and whether that work caused Mr. Sanchez's injury. (Bell Dec., pars. 5-6; Sanchez Depo., 39-40.) The court overrules DWC's objections to plaintiff's testimony as irrelevant, speculative and lacking foundation. Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, and its progeny does not bar liability as Mr. Sanchez creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether DWC was directly or affirmatively negligent. Put simply, if DWC was conducting excavation work in the area where Mr. Sanchez was working, DWC owed a duty to perform that work reasonably. The agreement between DWC and DPR does not bar Mr. Sanchez's claim. Even if DPR assumed all excavation safety responsibilities, which overstates the terms of the agreement, DWC does not cite any authority holding that such an agreement would bar Mr. Sanchez's claims. That appears to be a claim of contractual indemnity between DWC and DPR per Labor Code sec. 3864. The court does not find TIC Energy and Chemical, Inc. v. Martin (2016) 498 S.W.3d 68 apposite as that case relates to application of a particular Texas statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Sanchez is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Protect Medical Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF PAUL O'GRADY'S Motion To Protect Medical Records. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Local Rules state: "Failure to lodge courtesy copies of moving papers at least three days before a hearing or as otherwise required by statute, rule or court order may, in the discretion of the judicial officer presiding over the hearing, result in denial of the motion, continuance of the hearing, or taking the motion off calendar." S.F. Civ. L.R. 2.7(B). Further "Any counsel, party represented by counsel, or self represented litigant, who fails to comply with any of the requirements set forth in the rules will, upon motion of a party or the Court, be subject to the sanctions set forth in CCP § 575.2." S.F. Civ. L.R. 1.1. This motion is continued to September 4, 2018 to give Plaintiff Paul O'Grady ("O'Grady") the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B). O'Grady must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and reply papers (if any) to Judge Pro Tem B. Douglas Robbins at 1 Post St. Suite 800, San Francisco, CA 94104, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 3, 2018, at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per California Rules of Court 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OLATUNJI O RAHEEM VS. GOODFELLOW TOP GRADE CONSTRUCTION, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 3, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT GOODFELLOW TOP GRADE CONSTRUCTION, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Goodfellow Top Grade Construction, LLC's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether Goodfellow's failures to comply with Labor Code §226 were "knowing and intentional" rather than "isolated and unintentional." (See Kao v. Holiday (2017) 12 Cal.App.4th 947, 961-62.) This is particularly so as the failures apparently occurred some 1,500 times over a period of many months. (PMF 5-8.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)
